\doxysubsection{Detailed Description}
Minimal 3D BSP tree for triangle meshes. 

Usage\+:
\begin{DoxyItemize}
\item Add mesh elements as polygons via \doxylink{classBsp3D_af9a47ebf1f5e7e871fe73572c8b0678c}{Add\+Element()}.
\item Pick a splitter via \doxylink{classBsp3D_aa47770da171d55386ff3274412b58739}{Set\+Splitter()} (last element in the list).
\item Call \doxylink{classBsp3D_a214442602cb9f9276144dbe828ed793d}{Branch()} to recursively partition elements into inside/outside children until all leaves are marked as buds.
\item Use \doxylink{classBsp3D_a7f8ee74b1c8aff83c5a1cebf21356b54}{Test\+Point()} to classify query points (inside or outside). 
\end{DoxyItemize}

Definition at line \mbox{\hyperlink{Bsp3D_8h_source_l00044}{44}} of file \mbox{\hyperlink{Bsp3D_8h_source}{Bsp3\+D.\+h}}.

\Hypertarget{classBsp3D_a214442602cb9f9276144dbe828ed793d}\index{Bsp3D@{Bsp3D}!Branch@{Branch}}

\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{Branch()}{Branch()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{classBsp3D_a214442602cb9f9276144dbe828ed793d} 
bool Bsp3\+D\+::\+Branch (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}{}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Recursively branch on the current splitter and distribute elements. 

Creates {\ttfamily bsp\+Inside} and {\ttfamily bsp\+Outside} children, assigns/splits every pending polygon into the appropriate child list, then recurses. A child receiving no elements becomes a bud. \begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
Always {\ttfamily true}. 
\end{DoxyReturn}

Signed distance from a point to the splitter plane.

\hline

Select the last pushed element as splitter at this node.

\Hypertarget{classBsp3D_a53fed4500360085faceb956c49913117}\index{Bsp3D@{Bsp3D}!SplitPoly@{SplitPoly}}
\index{SplitPoly@{SplitPoly}!Bsp3D@{Bsp3D}}
\doxysubsubsection{\texorpdfstring{SplitPoly()}{SplitPoly()}}
{\footnotesize\ttfamily \label{classBsp3D_a53fed4500360085faceb956c49913117} 
bool Bsp3\+D\+::\+Split\+Poly (\begin{DoxyParamCaption}\item[{\mbox{\hyperlink{structbspPolygon}{bsp\+Polygon}} \texorpdfstring{$\ast$}{*}}]{poly}{}\end{DoxyParamCaption})}



Clip a polygon against the splitter, dispatching fragments to children. 

Evaluates all vertices against the splitter plane and, if the polygon straddles the plane, inserts intersection points and produces two new polygons\+: one for the inside child and one for the outside child. If the polygon lies fully on one side, it is forwarded intact.


\begin{DoxyParams}{Parameters}
{\em poly} & Polygon to classify/split (input and, if fully on one side, forwarded to a child). \\
\hline
\end{DoxyParams}
\begin{DoxyReturn}{Returns}
{\ttfamily true} if {\ttfamily poly} was split into two newly allocated polygons and the caller may delete the original; {\ttfamily false} if {\ttfamily poly} remains owned/needed by a child. 
\end{DoxyReturn}
